mnemonics: Don't Stop, Interrupt Instead: Use Thread.interrupt() as a flag, not Thread.stop() which is deprecated and unsafe.
The Polite Way Out: A thread should be asked to stop (via interruption) and then given a chance to clean up and terminate itself gracefully.
keyPoints: The Thread.stop() method is deprecated because it is inherently unsafe—it releases all monitors and can leave shared data in an inconsistent state.
The correct way to stop a thread is to use a cooperative mechanism where the thread periodically checks a flag (like the interrupted status) and cleans up before terminating.
A thread can check its interrupted status by calling Thread.interrupted() (which clears the status) or isInterrupted() (which does not clear it).
commonMistakes: Using the deprecated Thread.stop() method, which can cause application instability and corrupted data.
Not handling the InterruptedException properly within a thread, often by simply swallowing the exception without cleaning up or resetting the interrupt status.
Using a custom volatile boolean flag without also checking for thread interruption, missing a standard and well-supported way to signal stopping.
